danger_signals.visual_form: Undead monster variants of human characters; grotesque transformations (Ambrose becomes 'hulking ogre-like')
danger_signals.meaning: Family curse manifestation; supernatural threat escalation; permanent danger from cursed bloodline

danger_signals.visual_form: Benevolent priests transformed into 'murderous psychopaths'; behavioral inversion
danger_signals.meaning: Artifact corruption spreads danger; corrupting influence of demonic objects; loss of moral anchors

danger_signals.visual_form: Travel through 'other dimensions entirely'; shift between time periods and magical realms (Oneiros, Eternal Autumn)
danger_signals.meaning: Reality breach imminent; world boundaries weakening; supernatural incursion expanding

danger_signals.visual_form: 'Hideous, insectoid monstrosity'; mummified Celtic warrior crumbling to dust; ground bursting open; breaching walls of reality
danger_signals.meaning: Seal failure; apex threat arrival; world-ending catastrophe; final danger state

safety_signals.visual_form: Established ally, friend relationship, position in home estate
safety_signals.meaning: Deceptive safety marker; narrative subversion; trust betrayal visual cue (only recognizable retroactively)

safety_signals.visual_form: Ancient weapon retrieval from monastery; obtaining legendary artifact
safety_signals.meaning: Empowerment against primary threats; possession of counter-curse tool; narrative progression security

safety_signals.visual_form: Home base location; Jeremiah's mansion; familiar territory
safety_signals.meaning: Established safe zone (compromised by Jeremiah's actual allegiance)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ancient occult book; standing stones ritual site; family crest/estate
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Four siblings as undead monsters: Bethany (torturer), Aaron (ghost), Ambrose (ogre-transformation), Lizbeth (cursed form); Jeremiah (betrayer form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Covenant Estate; family catacombs; private torture chamber; island ritual stones; ancestral holdings

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious vestments; monastic architecture; Scythe of the Celts artifact
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Benevolent priests corrupted to murderous forms; visual inversion from holy to profane
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Medieval monastery; multiple sister locations worldwide; sacred sites

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Single antagonist with demonic power ambitions; enslaver of magical dimension inhabitants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Magical dimension Oneiros (controlled territory); areas of magical enslavement

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Celtic religious authority; druids; ancient ritual practitioners
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ancient standing stones; burial sites; Celtic king entombment ritual locations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Cursed Celtic warrior; demonic seal breaker
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Insectoid monstrosity; mummified reanimation; apex transformation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dimensional breach points; seal location (island); reality rupture zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Covenant Estate appears stable; Jeremiah presents as remorseful survivor; family appears dead/resolved
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs conduct normal activities; Jeremiah writes letters seeking aid; estate functions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Covenant siblings materialize as undead monsters; family catacombs reveal tortured remains; dimensional cracks appear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Siblings hunt Patrick; environment becomes hostile; monsters spawn in previously safe areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dimensional instability increases; travel through time and multiple realms becomes possible; reality appears fractured
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies grow more numerous; supernatural events accelerate; betrayals manifest (Jeremiah's true nature)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ground bursts open; mummified Celtic warrior emerges and crumbles; insectoid monstrosity breaches reality; environmental collapse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Apocalyptic final battle; reality mechanics fail; ultimate combat encounter initiated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Undying King defeated; Patrick leaves island 'in a daze'; mysterious figure appears to reclaim Gel'ziabar Stone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sequel hook introduced; cyclical threat pattern emerges; worldwide monastery network revelation suggests ongoing danger

environmental_storytelling.example: Monastery shifts from benevolent holy site to murderous psychopath den due to Scythe corruption; visual theology becomes weaponized horror
Covenant Estate layout reveals family dysfunction: Bethany's torture chamber imprisoning Aaron; catacombs holding dead siblings; private chambers hiding corruption

Standing stones serve as ritual nexus across time; ancient druidic burial connects to medieval monastery; Celtic king entombment links to modern curse; multiple realities accessible from single locations
Scythe of the Celts presence corrupts entire monastery brotherhood; Gel'ziabar Stone becomes focal point of betrayal and power-seeking; magical objects reshape morality of those near them
Covenant siblings' monstrous forms visually communicate curse severity; Ambrose's 'ogre-like' transformation reflects family degradation; ghost Aaron indicates incomplete death state
Jeremiah's mansion and journals create false safety narrative; catacombs suggest he's mourning family rather than orchestrating events; visual domesticity masks actual malevolence

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Travels through estate, time, and dimensions seeking answers; collects journals and memories; pursues Covenant siblings methodically
Hunt Patrick actively; possess monstrous forms; resist permanent death until specific weapon used; haunt estate grounds
Transforms from remorseful survivor to ambitious sorcerer; manipulates Patrick into killing siblings; demands Gel'ziabar Stone; attempts to absorb Undying King power
Shift from peaceful monks to 'murderous psychopaths'; guard Scythe artifact; attack on sight; murder-focused mindset
Pursues demonic power independently; enslaves Oneiros dimension inhabitants; taxes Patrick in combat; represents external corruption vector
Remains dormant until seal breaks; emerges as 'hideous, insectoid monstrosity'; engages in final world-ending battle; represents apex threat activation
Appears after victory; steals Gel'ziabar Stone; claims ongoing conflict with Patrick; suggests cyclical curse continuation

notes: Undying uses classic horror narrative inversion: safety markers (friendship, home, allies) are systematically revealed as unreliable. Visual communication relies heavily on transformation (humans→monsters, benevolent→murderous) to indicate corruption states. The game establishes a curse hierarchy: family curse→artifact corruption→dimensional instability→apocalyptic entity release. Faction identity in Undying is defined more by curse-state and moral inversion than by traditional warfare/territory markers. The Gel'ziabar Stone functions as primary manifestation object—whoever possesses it represents current world-state threat level. Environmental storytelling prioritizes betrayal revelation and temporal connection-making over environmental detail description in available source material.
